news.summary: Coinbase has introduced a novel open-source standard that leverages a previously overlooked status code to seamlessly incorporate stablecoin payments into websites and APIs. This innovation aims to streamline the process of integrating stablecoin transactions, providing more efficient and user-friendly payment options. This new standard is set to revolutionize the way stablecoin payments are integrated online, offering a more accessible and convenient method for conducting transactions.
news.headline: Coinbase revives forgotten web payment code, enabling stablecoin payments on websites & APIs through new open-source standard.

news.summary: Following a significant increase in value by 1,700% attributed to its investment in Solana, the company has announced plans to undergo a split, aiming to enhance liquidity and expand accessibility to investors.
news.headline: DeFi Development Corp plans 7-for-1 stock split post 1,700% Solana-fueled surge to enhance liquidity and widen investor reach.

news.summary: Sappi North America, Inc., a prominent provider of paper, packaging items, and pulp, unveiled a new study titled "The Touch Advantage" in collaboration with Clemson University. Set to debut on May 7 at LUXE PACK New York 2025, the research delves into the impact of touch on consumer choices and attitudes within the paper and packaging sector. The analysis focuses on multisensory packaging, emphasizing the importance of tactile components in influencing purchasing decisions.
news.headline: Sappi NA partners with Clemson for "The Touch Advantage" study on how touch impacts purchasing decisions in paper industry. Launch at LUXE PACK NY 2025.

news.summary: President Donald Trump made it clear during a press session at the Oval Office that he has no plans to lower the 145% tariff on Chinese imports. In response to a question about reducing tariffs, he firmly stated, "No." Earlier, in April, the Trump administration had announced reciprocal tariff exemptions for electronics like phones and computers from China and other nations. However, Trump rejected the idea of extending these exemptions to items such as car seats, underlining the importance of maintaining a straightforward tariff policy. These remarks were delivered at the swearing-in ceremony of David Perdue, the new U.S. ambassador to China. This announcement came shortly before an upcoming meeting with Chinese officials, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, and U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer. Foreign leaders had recommended... More details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Trump rejects tariff rollback, maintaining 145% tariff on Chinese imports. No exemptions for car seats. Meeting with China officials imminent.

news.summary: Nutrien Ltd. disclosed outcomes of its yearly shareholders meeting on May 7, 2025. The Meeting saw participation from 488,556,988 common shares, which is 74.48% of outstanding shares. The focus was on electing directors, with all 12 nominees successfully securing positions. Notable nominee Christopher M. Burley received 9 votes for and 0 against, showcasing strong support from shareholders. The company's performance and shareholder engagement indicate a positive outlook for Nutrien Ltd.
news.headline: Nutrien reports 488.5M shares (74.48% outstanding) represented at the 2025 annual meeting, elects 12 director nominees.

news.summary: The Caribbean Utilities Company, Ltd. announced its unaudited results for the first quarter of 2025. Highlights include approval of a capital investment plan allowing investments in grid hardening, customer affordability programs, and low-carbon energy projects. Improved fuel efficiency and decreasing fuel prices led to a 20% reduction in fuel factor charges for customers compared to Q1 2024. The company had a capital expenditure of $21.9 million, resulting in a net earnings increase to $7.6 million, up from $6.2 million in Q1 2024. There was also a 2% increase in kilowatt-hour sales. The quarterly dividend for Class A shares increased to $0.185 per share from $0.18 in Q1 2024. The company is focused on delivering affordable and environmentally beneficial investments for customers, ensuring reliable, safe, sustainable, and affordable energy.
news.headline: Caribbean Utilities Company, Ltd. reported $21.9 million in capital expenditure, $7.6 million in net earnings, and a 2% increase in kWh sales for Q1 2025.

news.summary: A biopharmaceutical company based in Cranbury, New Jersey, Palatin Technologies, Inc., has announced its intention to challenge the decision to delist the company. The company specializes in developing innovative drugs that target the melanocortin receptor system. This announcement was made on May 7, 2025.
news.headline: Palatin Technologies moving to OTC Pink after NYSE American delisting; requesting review of determination. May 7, 2025.

news.summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell stated that the pressure he faces from President Trump has not influenced the central bank's decisions. Powell made this statement during an appearance on Bloomberg's "Balance of Power: Late Edition." He emphasized that the Fed remains committed to its independent decision-making process despite external pressures. This assertion comes amidst continued scrutiny and criticism from President Trump regarding the Fed's interest rate policy. Powell's comments highlight the Fed's efforts to maintain autonomy and make decisions based on economic data and analysis rather than political influence.
news.headline: Powell: Trump's pressure has no impact, says source | Bloomberg.com

news.summary: The COVID-19 pandemic continues to impact the world, with over 5.6 million confirmed cases and approximately 350,000 deaths globally. Countries have implemented various restrictions and lockdown measures to control the spread of the virus. In the United States alone, there have been more than 1.7 million cases and over 100,000 deaths reported. Health officials emphasize the importance of social distancing, wearing masks, and frequent handwashing to prevent further transmission of the virus. As economies start to reopen, experts warn of a potential second wave of infections if precautions are not followed. The race for a vaccine is also ongoing, with several promising candidates in development. The coming months will be crucial in determining the trajectory of the pandemic.
news.headline: Concerns about Indian earnings lessen, contributing to a positive trend in stocks.

news.summary: The company has seen a remarkable 1,700% increase in value attributed to its investments in Solana. The firm announced plans for a split, aiming to improve liquidity and expand investor participation in the market.
news.headline: DeFi firm plans 7-for-1 stock split post 1,700% surge from Solana, aiming to enhance liquidity & attract more investors.

news.summary: Results from Cohort 4 of the ENSURE study reveal that patients previously treated with BRII-179 experienced quicker and more successful clearance of surface antigen at the end of treatment. This suggests a significant advantage compared to participants who had not received BRII-179 before. The data underscores the potential of BRII-179 in enhancing curative treatments for liver conditions.
news.headline: Brii Bio reveals EOT data from Cohort 4 of ENSURE study at EASL Congress 2025, highlighting BRII-179's role in advancing higher HBsAg loss.

news.summary: Binance founder Changpeng Zhao disclosed on a Farokh Radio podcast episode that he applied for a pardon from US President Donald Trump, despite earlier denying reports of seeking one. The application, submitted two weeks ago, followed rumors linking him to a potential business deal with the Trump family. Zhao, a convicted felon for money laundering, served four months in prison and paid a $50 million fine as part of a $4.3 billion settlement by Binance with US authorities. A pardon wouldn't remove his conviction but could permit him to hold a management role at Binance.US. Despite being a Binance shareholder, Zhao asserted he has no intention of resuming the CEO position. Post-release, he is focusing on advisory roles in countries like Pakistan and Kyrgyzstan, guiding on crypto regulations and blockchain implementations.
news.headline: Binance's CZ applied for Trump pardon after money laundering conviction, saying lawyers submitted request about 2 weeks ago. Source confirmed earlier reports on seeking pardon amid business deal discussions. Zhao contributed $50 million of $4.3 billion fine, stepped down as CEO. No plans to return as CEO. Advising on blockchain solutions in Pakistan and Kyrgyzstan.

news.summary: A new report reveals that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.2%, marking a decrease of 0.3% from the previous month. The report shows that the economy added 250,000 jobs in the last quarter, surpassing analysts' expectations. The manufacturing sector experienced the largest growth, with a 2.5% increase in production. Additionally, the report indicates that wage growth has remained steady at 3.5% annually. Overall, the labor market appears to be robust, reflecting positive economic trends.
news.headline: Fortuna releases Q1 2025 results with revenue up 15% to $100M.

news.summary: Hyundai Motor Group has announced its deployment of HCloud, its private cloud platform, in Equinix data centers worldwide to enhance customer satisfaction and elevate service standards for its vast base of over 10 million users. The Group's initiative aims to leverage Equinix's global infrastructure to deliver an enhanced user experience and optimize services across its operations.
news.headline: Hyundai partners with Equinix to deploy HCloud in data centers worldwide, aiming to improve customer experience for 10M+ users.

news.summary: American Hotel Income Properties REIT LP (AHIP) based in Vancouver, British Columbia, is set to unveil its financial results for the quarter ending March 31, 2025, after the market closes on Wednesday, May 14, 2025. Those interested can join the Q1 2025 results conference call on Thursday, May 15, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. Pacific Time. The call will feature AHIP executives providing insights into the company's financial performance and addressing queries from analysts. AHIP trades on the Toronto Stock Exchange under the tickers HOT.UN, HOT.U, and HOT.DB.V.
news.headline: American Hotel Income Properties REIT LP will release Q1 2025 results on May 14, 2025, with conference call on May 15 at 10:00 a.m. PT.

news.summary: The Labrador Iron Ore Royalty Corporation's first quarter report for 2025 shows a decline in financial performance due to lower sales tonnages and iron ore prices. Royalty revenue was $35.6 million, 36% lower than 2024, with net income per share at $0.33, a 64% decrease. Sales tonnages were down 26%, with a drop in pellet and CFS sales significantly affecting revenue. IOC concentrate production decreased by 10% compared to 2024. Iron ore prices were down, with the 65% Fe index averaging $117/tonne. The outlook remains uncertain due to geopolitical developments. Royalty revenue was $35.6 million, with net income per share at $0.33. IOC's concentrate production decreased by 10% compared to 2024, and overall sales tonnages were down 26%. Iron ore prices were lower in the first quarter of 2025, with the 65% Fe index averaging $117 per tonne. The outlook for the second quarter is uncertain due to geopolitical developments. The Corporation had a positive net working capital of $22 million as of March 31, 2025.
news.headline: Labrador Iron Ore Royalty Corporation's first quarter report for the period ended March 31, 2025, showed a decrease in revenue to $36.2 million, net income at $21.4 million, and cash flow per share to $0.39 due to lower sales tonnages and iron ore prices. IOC sales tonnage dropped 26%, with CFS tonnages down 43% compared to the previous year. Iron ore price averaged US$117 per tonne in Q1 2025, 14% lower than Q1 2024. Cash flow was 37% lower than Q1 2024. The outlook for iron ore pricing in Q2 remained uncertain due to geopolitical developments involving US tariffs.
